title: Boronia muelleri
taxonomy: Boronia muelleri is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. This evergreen shrub is native to the southwestern region of Western Australia. Within the genus Boronia, B. muelleri is part of the Boronieae tribe. The family Rutaceae, to which B. muelleri belongs, is commonly known as the citrus or rue family. The classification of B. muelleri reflects its botanical relationships within the flowering plant kingdom.

intro: Boronia muelleri, also known as the Mountain Boronia, is a small, flowering plant native to Australia. The health benefits of Boronia muelleri include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate anxiety and stress, and provide antioxidant properties, making it a promising natural remedy for various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Boronia muelleri include flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, as well as essential oils containing monoterpenes and sesquiterpenes.
